text:
Father’s Day has always been tough for me since I lost my dad when I was 19 but this year feels especially heavy because its also my first Father’s Day as a dad.

This photo was taken nearly 30 years ago on Father’s Day and while I wish I could share with him some of the moments I’ve already had with my son, I’m grateful for all the moments he gave me growing up that I’ll always remember and now get to create for my son which is something I don't take for granted.

Happy Father’s Day to all the dads out there who are constantly learning, growing and doing everything they can to build a better life for their families.

This is Peter Lynch’s favorite metric and I like it too because it ties valuation to growth. I prefer using a 2026 to 2028 window so the multiple adjusts for how fast the business is actually compounding.

For example $SOFI may trade at ~27x forward earnings but earnings are expected to grow from $0.60 in 2026 to $1.03 in 2028 implying a ~30% earnings CAGR and a PEG of ~0.9x.

POPULAR STOCKS BY PEG RATIO

PEG < 1 usually means mispriced growth
PEG > 2 starts to push into the danger zone

Here’s how they stack up now: 
• $SPCX ~4.7x
• $TSLA ~4.6x

MY 2030 $NBIS TARGET PRICE

I initiated a position in Nebius last year around $30 as one of my favorite ways to play the next stage of AI where the focus shifts from scaling compute to delivering reliable AI outcomes at the right cost.

As agentic AI continues to scale the infrastructure, utilization and cost per token become far more important which is exactly where I think Nebius is positioned. 

Thats why my 2030 base case can look aggressive at first glance because it assumes demand is moving structurally toward high-volume inference workloads where capacity, utilization and efficiency matter more than ever: 

Revenue
• 2026 ~$3.3B (+505% YoY)
• 2027 ~$9.8B (+199% YoY)
• 2028 ~$16.9B (+72% YoY)
• 2029 ~$23.4B (+39% YoY)
• 2030 ~$26.9B (+15% YoY)

EBITDA
• 2026 ~$1.4B (+2300% YoY)
• 2027 ~$4.9B (+252% YoY)
• 2028 ~$8.9B (+82% YoY)
• 2029 ~$13.9B (+57% YoY)
• 2030 ~$16.4B (+18% YoY)

Nebius would be worth ~$250B or around $850 per share (4% annual dilution) if stock trades at 15x 2030 EBITDA.

$MU going from ~$1B to ~$160B in operating income in less than 3 years is outrageous growth.

AI compute supply is no longer just filling existing demand but its creating it as bigger GPU & HBM clusters unlock better models, more agents & far more compute-intensive use cases.

Jim Simons first disclosed $NVDA in Q1 2011 (now +46,000%) and $MU in Q3 2013 (now +6,000%).

The quant firm he built (Renaissance Technologies) now holds a ~$1B stake in $PLTR which is trying to own the execution layer of real-world AI as agentic AI scales.

If a store of value goes through cycles, that means it’s not storing value properly in those cycles, WHICH MEANS IT ISN’T A STORE OF VALUE GOODNIGHT

For a decade, Gold went sideways.

It did not preserve purchasing power.

Please define short term.

As I’ve said repeatedly - there is no such thing as a true store of value.

A store of value must be predictable.

The moment there are tails in a distribution, it isn’t predictable.

It’s a made up marketing term to get naive people to over allocate.
When I agree with you I know I’ve completely lost my mind. But I guess desperate times….
Yes because A STORE OF VALUE CANT FUCKING CRASH HOLY SHIT AT THE READING COMPREHENSION LEVEL
TAKE MY FUCKING MONEY

Someone literally just said that a store of value has cycles.

HOW

THE

FUCK

CAN

A

STORE

OF

VALUE

HAVE

CYCLES

!?!?!?!?

This is a comment someone put to my pointy hat diversification means investing in something that you hate.

What will you hate in your portfolio?

The investment that isn’t going up the moment you look at it.

Which means the investment that is uncorrelated.
